evidence_label: compact PubMed response-capture refresh, not treatment advice
query: (thalassemia) AND (HBG1 OR HBG2 OR fetal hemoglobin OR BCL11A OR HBS1L-MYB OR DNMT1 OR erythroid) AND 2026[pdat]

records.role: erythroid metabolism context; supports keeping model and endpoint detail explicit in lab replies
records.title: Understanding and targeting erythroid cell metabolism.

records.role: HBG promoter-editing benchmark; supports keeping HbF-only quote replies insufficient
records.title: CRISPR-Cas12a Gene Editing of HBG1 and HBG2 Promoters to Treat beta-Thalassemia.

records.role: non-editing HbF pharmacology signal; high-caution benchmark context only
records.title: Discovery of a Novel DNMT1 Inhibitor with Improved Efficacy in Treating beta-Thalassemia.

records.role: gamma-globin regulation context; reinforces that HbF signals need mechanism and safety context
records.title: p27Kip1 regulates gamma-globin production.

records.role: genome-editing delivery benchmark; not a first-quote action route
records.title: In vivo genome editing of human haematopoietic stem cells for treatment of blood disorders using mRNA delivery.

records.role: clinical-trial editing review context; benchmark only, not lab-quote routing
records.title: CRISPR/Cas9 System as a Promising Therapy in Thalassemia and Sickle Cell Disease: A Systematic Review of Clinical Trials.

decision_use: The June 13 check supports strict response capture: a lab reply must be reduced to public-safe model, endpoint, missing-field, boundary, cost/timeline, and next-state labels before any repo update. The source refresh does not authorize lab outreach or clinical action.
